body_txt: A Zombie Apocalypse is no fun for anyone; even the zombies are incapable of feeling fun. It's especially hard for the Zombie Infectee; carelessly bitten by a zombie or infected with an early stage of The Virus , they're in for a slow and painful transformation into a monster that will kill or convert their friends and loved ones. The only way out is death, either by suicide or mercy kill , and any miracle cure or even Heroic Willpower is right out the window if you aren't a main character. So what's a bite-ee to do? Nothing. (No, seriously.) Any group trying to survive in this apocalyptic situation will always have at least one idiot Jerkass who gets bitten or scratched and refuses to face the truth (traumatic as it is) and tell anyone , knowing full well that their silence will cost lives . They don't seek help because they know there is nothing for their condition but a bullet to the head. Yet they also won't isolate from the group so they will not endanger the others, let alone attempt to go out in a Heroic Sacrifice or at least spend their last hours trying to do some good . The Zombie Infectee is primarily (and in their defense, rightfully,) afraid their friends will kill them once the truth is out. Fear of discovery means they live their now-shortened lives terrified and in denial, and they end up behaving irrationally because of it. Alternatively, they will desperately cling to the hope that they are the one person immune to The Virus , despite it having a 100% fatality and 100% conversion rate — in the most headstrong cases, they may try to Resist the Beast . At this point, unfortunately, the onus is on the heroes to notice the Infectee's erratic behavior (for a Zombie Apocalypse , anyway) and Shoot the Dog . Other people — friends, relatives, lovers — may also sink into denial and try to hinder the heroes from dispatching this walking liability before they become the walking dead. But there's no point: the Zombie Infectee inevitably turns and subsequently infects or kills at least one more victim (often the Zombie Advocate , for additional tragic irony ), unleashes the horde of zombies , destroys all the ammunition, or finds some other way to cause a really bad day for the remaining survivors. Note: A few zombie works (movies, literature, etc.) have taken a third option, so to speak. If the point of infection is near the end of a limb (which it often is), that limb can then be removed . The person may die of shock and blood loss anyway, but at least he's not getting back up again to snack on your brains. Hopefully. Also, oftentimes (Especially more recently, thanks to how saturated the genre is) it's not a death sentence . In that case , they will tell everyone thanks to the ubiquity of this trope in fiction , and then die . Obviously, much of this page talks about works in which a Zombie Infectee is kept a secret even from the audience, and thus learning the identity of one prior to experiencing the work would ruin a somewhat major twist . Beware of spoilers from here on. They may or may not be the first infectee of an upcoming zombie apocalypse; for situations like these see Patient Zero . See also And Then John Was a Zombie and Vampire Refugee . Compare and contrast Secret Stab Wound and Mortal Wound Reveal . Most examples are also Secretly Dying .
